chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: Let's say you are writing a python program.  You have to use a particular version of a package.  When that package is updated, your friend is using another version.  You are using another version.  In this case, you use 50 such packages in your project.

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: How will you maintain the version?  How will you ensure that the packages that your program is using,  your friend should use exactly the same versions.  And how will you distribute your program?  Let's go to the computer screen.

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: We will see about virtual environment,  which makes this task easy for us.  It makes an isolated environment.  Which is completely different from your global python interpreter.  And you can install your own packages in it.

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: Let's get started.  So, the exact use case of virtual environment,  first of all, I will tell you that.  And after that, we will make a virtual environment.  So, it will be a little clearer to you.

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: What exactly is a virtual environment?  And what it is all about?  So, first of all, you guys come to my rappel.  And see what I have written here.  A virtual environment is a tool used to isolate

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: specific python environments on a single machine.  Allowing you to work on multiple projects with different dependencies  and packages without conflicts.  Okay?  What is this?

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: It basically says that,  virtual environment helps you to separate your python environment.  For example, I am using version 1 of pandas.  My friend is using version 2 of pandas.  And suppose that,

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: because version 1 is installed in my computer,  how will I run his project?  Will I run my project by uninstalling my version?  So, what can I do now?  I will make a virtual environment for version 1 of pandas.

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: I will make a virtual environment for version 2 of pandas.  Okay?  This was an example.  Now see here, this can be especially useful when working on projects.  That have conflicting package versions

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: or packages that are not compatible with each other.  Okay?  Now see, this is also a very important point.  Packages that are not compatible with each other.  Suppose I have a package.

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: Package A.  And it depends on package B.  On package B version 1.  Suppose I update package A.  And now it starts depending on package B version 2.

chunks.title: Virtual Environment in Python
chunks.text: So, basically what I have to do?  I have to pick up an environment.  In that, whatever packages I want, I can install.  And those packages' versions will not have any meaning with my Python installed version.  Okay?

chunks.text: If you didn't understand, then I will just tell you.  How is it installed?  How is it made?  So, first of all, what you have to do?  A package named virtual environment.
